title: PhD Scholarship in Education 2026 at Canterbury Christ Church University: Fully Funded Opportunity on Education Values and Impact
summary: The Canterbury Christ Church University (CCCU) is offering an exciting PhD Scholarship in Education: Education Values and Impact for 2026. This fully funded opportunity is designed for candidates passionate about exploring how education shapes values among children and young people and how these values influence society across generations. This scholarship is ideal for individuals interested in education research, youth engagement, social action, and intergenerational dialogue , with a strong emphasis on innovative and creative research methodologies. Scholarship Overview This PhD project focuses on understanding how education can create meaningful spaces where children and young people engage with: Civic values Political values Social values The research will also investigate how these values are perceived across different generations and whether commonly discussed societal divides are real or influenced by a “values perception gap.” The project builds on existing research, including insights from Dr Emily Lau , and aligns with studies highlighting how young people’s engagement in social action is shaped by compassion and societal expectations. Research Focus Areas The successful candidate will contribute to cutting-edge research exploring: The values perception gap between generations The role of education in shaping social and political attitudes How schools and community programs foster dialogue and understanding The impact of subjects like PSHE, Citizenship, and Religious Education The role of charities and community organizations in education The project also encourages creative and visual research methods , particularly when working with children and young people. What Does the Scholarship Offer? Successful applicants will receive a comprehensive funding package, including: 💷 Annual stipend: £21,805 🎓 Full tuition fee waiver for 3 years 📊 Annual research allowance: £500 👩‍🏫 Opportunity to gain teaching experience (150 hours across years 2 and 3) Beyond financial support, scholars will engage in impactful research that contributes to academic knowledge and real-world educational practices. Eligibility Criteria To apply for this PhD scholarship, candidates must: Hold an undergraduate degree and a Master’s (MA) in Education or a related field Demonstrate strong academic ability and commitment to research Have experience working with children and young people (essential) Show interest or background in community engagement, social or climate action, or education Meet English language requirements (e.g., IELTS if applicable) Be able to live in the UK within commuting distance of CCCU Be available to start full-time in September 2026 Applicants must also apply for both the PhD programme and the scholarship simultaneously . Application Process To apply, candidates should follow these steps: Contact a potential supervisor at Canterbury Christ Church University to discuss research ideas Download and complete the research proposal form Submit an online application through the university portal Upload required documents: Research proposal CV Academic references (2) Proof of qualifications IELTS results (if required) Shortlisted candidates may be invited for an interview with the academic panel. Application Deadline 📌 Deadline: 5 June 2026 Applicants are encouraged to prepare early, especially when developing a strong and well-aligned research proposal. Why Apply for This PhD? This scholarship offers more than academic funding—it provides an opportunity to: Contribute to important debates on education and societal values Work with experienced researchers and academic mentors Develop innovative research approaches with real-world impact Explore intergenerational dialogue and social change For those passionate about education, youth engagement, and social impact, this PhD is a powerful step toward a meaningful academic and professional career. For more information click here Discover more opportunities here The post PhD Scholarship in Education 2026 at Canterbury Christ Church University: Fully Funded Opportunity on Education Values and Impact appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Elephant Conservation & Research Funding 2027: Apply for Grants from the International Elephant Foundation
summary: The International Elephant Foundation (IEF) has officially opened applications for its Elephant Conservation and Research Funding Support Program 2027 , offering critical financial support to projects dedicated to protecting elephants, preserving their habitats, and supporting the communities that coexist with them. This funding opportunity is a major initiative for conservationists, researchers, NGOs, and field practitioners working to address the urgent challenges facing elephant populations worldwide. Overview of the Elephant Conservation Grant Program Each year, the International Elephant Foundation supports 20 to 25 projects globally , providing funding to initiatives that demonstrate clear impact, sustainability, and innovation in elephant conservation. The program focuses on practical, field-based solutions that are: Scientifically sound Ethically and culturally appropriate Feasible and scalable Inclusive of local stakeholders Applicants are expected to demonstrate that their proposed methods are either proven effective or strongly supported by research. Projects based on untested or ineffective strategies are unlikely to be funded. Funding Details Successful applicants can expect: 💵 Average grant award: $15,000 per year 🌍 Funding for both annual and multi-year projects 📊 Support for projects with measurable conservation impact The funding is designed to empower initiatives that can deliver tangible outcomes within a relatively short timeframe, while also building long-term sustainability. Priority Areas for Funding The program prioritizes projects that address key challenges in elephant conservation, including: Human-elephant conflict prevention and mitigation Habitat conservation and ecosystem management Community education and outreach programs Transboundary (cross-border) conservation efforts Anti-poaching initiatives and wildlife protection systems Reducing ivory and bushmeat trade Research on elephant health and diseases Projects that combine multiple focus areas and demonstrate systemic impact are particularly encouraged. What Makes a Strong Application? The International Elephant Foundation prioritizes proposals that: Are community-based with potential for growth Deliver immediate and measurable results Can be evaluated within 1–2 years Are led by experienced investigators with proven field capacity Show strong collaboration with local stakeholders Applicants are strongly advised to prepare thoroughly and align their proposals with these expectations to increase their chances of success. What the Grant Does NOT Cover To ensure funding is used effectively, certain expenses are not eligible, including: International travel to project sites Purchase of weapons or ammunition Paid informants Tuition fees for advanced degrees Overhead or indirect costs Applicants should carefully structure their budgets to comply with these guidelines. Application Process To apply, candidates must: Complete the online application form in English Prepare responses using the provided Word template (recommended) Upload all required supporting documents Submit the application before the deadline IEF also provides helpful resources, including: Grant-writing workshop recordings Presentation slides Sample application templates These tools are designed to guide applicants through the process and improve proposal quality. Required Supporting Documents Applicants must submit several key documents, including: Project location images (country and specific site) Photos of elephants in the project area Budget and timeline spreadsheets Organizational recognition documents (if applicable) CVs of investigators and co-investigators Permits, endorsements, and approvals Ethical clearance documents (e.g., IACUC, if applicable) Providing complete and well-prepared documentation is essential for a competitive application. Application Deadline 📌 Deadline: Friday, August 7, 2026 (11:59 PM PST) Late submissions will not be considered, so applicants are encouraged to start early and ensure all materials are complete before submission. Why This Grant Matters Elephants play a crucial role in maintaining biodiversity and ecosystem balance. However, they face increasing threats from habitat loss, poaching, and human-wildlife conflict. By supporting impactful conservation projects, the International Elephant Foundation is helping to: Protect endangered elephant populations Promote sustainable coexistence between humans and wildlife Strengthen conservation capacity worldwide For researchers and conservation practitioners, this grant represents a meaningful opportunity to contribute to global environmental protection while advancing their professional work. For more information click here Discover more opportunities here The post Elephant Conservation Research Funding 2027: Apply for Grants from the International Elephant Foundation appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Energy Transition Fund Jobs 2026: Programme Officer Roles in Latin America & Africa (Remote, High-Impact Climate Careers)
summary: The Energy Transition Fund (ETF) is currently offering two high-impact career opportunities for professionals passionate about climate action and global energy transformation. These Programme Officer roles focus on accelerating a just and equitable transition away from fossil fuels in key regions: Latin America the Caribbean and Africa . These full-time, remote positions provide a unique opportunity to work at the intersection of climate policy, philanthropy, and global development , contributing to systemic change aligned with the Paris Agreement . About the Energy Transition Fund (ETF) Founded in 2019, the Energy Transition Fund is a collaborative funding initiative dedicated to supporting strategies that enable a rapid and fair transition away from oil and gas . ETF operates globally and works behind the scenes with philanthropic partners, civil society actors, and research institutions to drive meaningful climate action. Despite its low public profile, ETF is a growing and influential organization, offering professionals the chance to engage in impactful, large-scale climate strategies. Position 1: Latin America Caribbean Programme Officer 📍 Location: Mexico (Remote Work) 📄 Job Type: Permanent, Full-Time 🎯 Role Overview The Latin America Caribbean Programme Officer will lead strategic efforts to accelerate the energy transition in: Mexico (primary focus) Argentina (secondary focus) The role involves designing and implementing grantmaking strategies, managing partnerships, and identifying opportunities to strengthen climate action ecosystems across the region. Key Responsibilities Develop and manage a grantmaking portfolio in Mexico and Argentina Identify, evaluate, and support grantee organizations Build strong partnerships with stakeholders, funders, and civil society actors Lead cross-functional collaboration across ETF teams Monitor political, economic, and policy developments affecting energy transition Support fundraising, reporting, and strategic planning Required Expertise Strong understanding of energy policy, climate strategy, or economic transition Ability to build coalitions and drive systemic change Experience in philanthropy or grantmaking (preferred) Excellent communication skills in English and Spanish APPLY HERE Position 2: Africa Programme Officer 📍 Location: Nigeria (Remote Work) 📄 Job Type: Permanent, Full-Time 🎯 Role Overview The Africa Programme Officer will lead ETF’s work in: Nigeria (primary focus) Senegal (secondary focus) This role mirrors the Latin America position but focuses on African energy transition dynamics, supporting regional strategies and partnerships. Key Responsibilities Build and manage a grantmaking portfolio in Nigeria and Senegal Strengthen partnerships with civil society and philanthropic actors Provide expertise on regional energy and policy landscapes Identify funding opportunities and strategic risks Support collaboration across regional and global stakeholders Required Expertise Deep knowledge of African energy, policy, or civil society landscapes Experience in strategy, finance, or climate-related work Strong communication skills in English and French Ability to manage complex, multi-stakeholder projects Candidate Profile (Both Roles) ETF is seeking professionals who are: Holders of an undergraduate degree or equivalent experience Equipped with at least 5 years of relevant professional experience Strategic thinkers with strong analytical and communication skills Able to manage multiple projects and meet deadlines Comfortable working in remote, global, and multicultural teams Experience in philanthropy, grantmaking, or climate advocacy is highly valued. Compensation Benefits Both roles offer: 💵 Salary range: $81,553 – $91,677 USD (depending on experience) 🌍 Fully remote work environment 🧳 Opportunities for international travel 🖥️ Equipment and office support 🤝 Collaborative, inclusive global team culture Work Environment ETF operates as a globally distributed organization , with most staff working remotely. Employees benefit from: Flexible working hours Optional office reimbursements Exposure to international networks and high-level policy discussions The organization emphasizes professionalism, inclusivity, and compassion , creating a supportive environment for impactful work. Application Deadlines Latin America Caribbean Programme Officer: 18 May 2026 Africa Programme Officer: 20 May 2026 Why These Roles Matter These positions offer more than employment—they are an opportunity to: Influence global climate strategies Work with leading philanthropic and policy actors Contribute to a just and equitable energy transition Build a meaningful, purpose-driven international career For professionals committed to tackling climate change at a systemic level, ETF provides a rare and powerful platform. Discover more opportunities here The post Energy Transition Fund Jobs 2026: Programme Officer Roles in Latin America Africa (Remote, High-Impact Climate Careers) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: McKenzie Postdoctoral Fellowships 2027 at the University of Melbourne: Fully Funded Opportunity for Early Career Researchers Worldwide
summary: The McKenzie Postdoctoral Fellowships Scheme 2027 is one of the most prestigious early-career research programs offered by the University of Melbourne . Established in 2010, this flagship initiative is designed to support exceptional postdoctoral researchers at a critical stage in their academic journey, helping them build research independence, expand collaborations, and strengthen their leadership capabilities in academia. This highly competitive fellowship offers a unique opportunity for talented scholars from around the world to conduct advanced research in a dynamic, interdisciplinary environment while contributing to the University’s broader research agenda. Overview of the McKenzie Postdoctoral Fellowships The McKenzie Fellowship is structured as a three-year, full-time (1.0 FTE) research-focused program . It provides recipients with dedicated time and resources to: Advance their research projects Develop innovative ideas and collaborations Enhance academic leadership and mentorship skills Contribute to institutional and global research priorities Each year, up to 16 fellowships are awarded across a wide range of disciplines aligned with the University’s research strengths. Importantly, the scheme also reflects a strong commitment to equity, diversity, and inclusion . In line with the University’s Indigenous Strategy Murmuk Djerring , at least two fellowships are reserved for Indigenous researchers or those whose work supports inclusive academic development . Funding and Benefits The McKenzie Postdoctoral Fellowship offers a comprehensive funding package that supports both the fellow and their research activities: Competitive salary at Academic Level A6 (including superannuation and employment benefits) Up to AUD $25,000 research grant for project-related expenses over the fellowship duration Full institutional support from the host faculty Access to world-class research infrastructure and networks Opportunities for mentorship and career development This funding structure ensures that fellows can focus fully on producing high-quality, impactful research. Eligibility Criteria To be eligible for the McKenzie Postdoctoral Fellowship, applicants must meet the following key requirements: Hold a PhD in a relevant discipline from any recognized university worldwide, including the University of Melbourne Have completed all PhD requirements on or after 1 April 2023 Be an early career researcher (ECR) with strong academic potential Both Australian and international applicants are eligible Additionally, current University of Melbourne academic staff may apply if they are employed under casual, fixed-term, or research-contingent contracts. Applicants who do not strictly meet eligibility requirements may still apply by submitting an Eligibility Exemption Request (EER) , provided they can demonstrate valid exceptional circumstances such as career interruptions. Academic Contact Requirement A distinctive aspect of this fellowship is the requirement to secure an Academic Contact within the University. This individual plays a crucial role by: Supporting and endorsing the application Providing informal mentoring during the fellowship Helping integrate the fellow into the University’s research environment Applicants must submit a signed Academic Contact Form as part of their Expression of Interest (EOI). Missing or unsigned forms will result in disqualification. Application Process (Step-by-Step) The application process is divided into two main stages: 1. Expression of Interest (EOI) Applicants must: Review official guidelines carefully Identify and confirm an Academic Contact Prepare required documents, including project summaries Submit the EOI via SmartyGrants Only highly ranked EOIs will proceed to the next stage. 2. Full Application (by invitation only) Shortlisted candidates must: Complete a detailed research proposal Submit referee testimonials via email Provide all required supporting documentation Faculty-Specific Requirements Each faculty within the University of Melbourne may have additional submission requirements or internal deadlines. Applicants are strongly encouraged to contact their target faculty early to ensure compliance. Fields include: Arts and Social Sciences Engineering and IT Medicine, Dentistry and Health Sciences Business and Economics Law Science Fine Arts Music Education Architecture and Planning Some faculties may require early registration of interest, internal approvals, or participation in support sessions. Important Dates (2026–2027 Cycle) 1 April 2026 – Applications open 16 April 2026 – ECR Fellowships webinar 7 May 2026 (3:00 PM) – EOI submission deadline 22 July 2026 – EOI results announced 19 August 2026 (3:00 PM) – Full application deadline End of October 2026 – Final results announced 30 June 2027 – अंतिम start date for fellows Why Apply for the McKenzie Fellowship? The McKenzie Postdoctoral Fellowship is more than just funding—it is a career-defining opportunity . Fellows benefit from: Global academic exposure Interdisciplinary collaboration opportunities Structured mentorship Long-term career advancement support For researchers aiming to transition into independent academic roles, this fellowship provides the ideal platform to build a strong and impactful research portfolio. For more information click here Discover more opportunities here The post McKenzie Postdoctoral Fellowships 2027 at the University of Melbourne: Fully Funded Opportunity for Early Career Researchers Worldwide appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Macquarie LEADS High School Externship 2026
summary: Applications are now open for the Macquarie LEADS High School Externship , offered by Macquarie Group in partnership with Extern . This program is a great opportunity for high school students interested in exploring careers in financial services. About the Externship The externship is a 5-week hybrid programme designed to give students early exposure to the financial services industry through hands-on learning and mentorship. Participants will engage with professionals, gain industry insights, and build foundational skills. Key Details Programme: Macquarie LEADS High School Externship Duration: July 6 – August 7, 2026 (5 weeks) Format: Hybrid Locations: Houston, Jacksonville, and New York City Application Deadline: May 31, 2026 Who Can Apply This opportunity is open to: High school juniors (Class of 2027) Students interested in financial services and business careers Benefits of the Programme Gain early exposure to the financial services industry Develop professional and career-readiness skills Learn directly from industry professionals Build networks and mentorship connections Opportunity to receive a $1,000 educational stipend upon successful completion Why This Opportunity Matters This externship provides a strong foundation for students considering careers in finance, offering real-world exposure and valuable connections at an early stage. How to Apply Apply here. For more Internship opportunities from OFY, Click Here. The post Macquarie LEADS High School Externship 2026 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: UNDP Youth for Climate Action Course 2026: Free Online Certificate Program Empowering Global Youth Leaders in Climate Advocacy and Policy
summary: The UNDP Youth for Climate Action Course 2026 is a globally accessible, free online learning program designed to equip young people with the knowledge, tools, and skills needed to understand climate change and actively contribute to solutions at local, national, and international levels. Developed by the United Nations Development Programme (UNDP) in partnership with Eco House Global and delivered through the Learning for Nature platform, this course represents a powerful opportunity for youth worldwide to become informed climate leaders and advocates. With climate change posing unprecedented risks to future generations, this course places young people at the center of climate action by providing them with structured, practical, and globally relevant learning. Overview of the Youth for Climate Action Course The Youth for Climate Action course is a self-paced, open-access digital program tailored to young people interested in climate change, sustainability, and policy engagement. It is designed “by youth, for youth,” ensuring that the content is relatable, inclusive, and action-oriented. Key Course Details Duration: 5 modules Format: Self-paced online course Languages: English and Spanish Estimated Effort: Approximately 1.5 hours per module Certification: Yes (upon completion) Platform: Learning for Nature Why This Course is Important Climate change is one of the most pressing global challenges of our time. Research indicates that young people today may experience significantly more extreme climate events than previous generations. Despite this reality, youth are often underrepresented in climate decision-making processes. This course aims to: Bridge the knowledge gap Empower youth participation in climate governance Promote inclusive and informed climate advocacy Strengthen global youth collaboration Course Objectives By enrolling in this course, participants will: Gain foundational knowledge of climate science, including causes and impacts Understand climate action strategies such as mitigation and adaptation Learn how climate governance works at international, national, and local levels Explore opportunities for civic engagement and advocacy Develop the ability to participate in climate negotiations and policy processes Course Structure and Modules The program is structured into five comprehensive modules: Module 1: The Science of Climate Change Core principles of climate science Causes and global impacts Module 2: Mitigation Strategies to reduce greenhouse gas emissions Sustainable solutions Module 3: Adaptation and Vulnerability Understanding climate risks Building resilience in vulnerable communities Module 4: International Governance and Cooperation Global climate agreements Policy frameworks and cooperation mechanisms Module 5: Advocacy and Participation by Civil Society Tools for civic engagement Influencing climate policy and action Course Highlights and Learning Outcomes Participants can expect to: Build practical skills for climate advocacy Learn from materials developed by leading global institutions Engage with real-world climate policy scenarios Connect with a global community of learners Earn a certificate of completion Partners and Contributors This course is backed by a strong network of global institutions, including: United Nations Development Programme (UNDP) Eco House Global UNICEF Latin America and the Caribbean Youth Negotiators Academy The Climate Reality Project Ministry of the Environment of Panama These organizations bring extensive expertise in climate education, policy, and advocacy. Benefits of the Youth for Climate Action Course Participants will benefit from: Free Access No cost to enroll Flexible Learning Self-paced modules Certification Earn a recognized completion certificate Global Networking Engage with youth worldwide Practical Skills Development Learn advocacy, policy, and leadership skills Real-World Impact Apply knowledge to community-based initiatives Course Completion Requirements To successfully complete the course and receive a certificate, participants must: Complete all five modules Pass quizzes at the end of each module Complete the final course survey Enrollment and Registration Process Interested participants can enroll easily: Visit the Learning for Nature platform Register for an account Enroll in the Youth for Climate Action course Begin learning immediately Application Deadline There is no fixed deadline for this course. Enrollment is open year-round, allowing participants to join at any time. Click HERE to learn more and apply to the UNDP Youth for Climate Action Course 2026. See the FAQs HERE Why You Should Enroll This course is not just an academic opportunity—it is a gateway to becoming an active participant in global climate solutions. By enrolling, you will: Understand climate challenges in depth Gain tools to advocate for change Join a global movement of youth leaders Strengthen your academic and professional profile Conclusion The UNDP Youth for Climate Action Course 2026 stands out as a highly impactful, accessible, and globally relevant opportunity for young people who are passionate about climate change and sustainability. Whether you are a student, activist, or aspiring policymaker, this course provides the knowledge, skills, and platform needed to drive meaningful change. Discover more Climate Action opportunities and Courses on OFY. title: FEA Youth Steering Group – Applications Open
summary: Applications are now open for the next cohort of the FEA Youth Steering Group , a leadership opportunity for young advocates passionate about advancing education equity. About the Programme The Youth Steering Group empowers young people to influence education policy, advocate for fairer systems, and shape conversations around educational access and equity. Previous cohort members have: Engaged in a roundtable with the Prime Minister Contributed to policy discussions with the Department for Education Taken on leadership roles across the education sector Who Should Apply This opportunity is for young people who: Are aged 14–21 Are passionate about education equity Have experienced barriers to fair education Want to use their voice to advocate for change Key Details Programme: FEA Youth Steering Group Application Deadline: 28 June 2026 Benefits of Joining Develop leadership and advocacy skills Influence education policy and sector conversations Build networks with peers and decision-makers Gain experience in public speaking and representation Why This Opportunity Matters This programme gives young people a platform to shape the future of education and ensure lived experiences inform policy and practice. title: Climate Justice Camp 2026 Asia in the Philippines: Fully Funded Regional Gathering for Climate Leaders and Community Organizers
summary: The Climate Justice Camp 2026 Asia is set to take place in Cebu, Philippines, from August 12 to 16, 2026, bringing together grassroots organizers, activists, and community leaders from across Asia and the Pacific. This transformative initiative is one of the largest in-person organizing platforms globally, designed to empower individuals working at the intersection of climate justice, human rights, and social equity. Launched in 2022, the Climate Justice Camp has rapidly grown into a global movement, with nearly 2,000 participants from over 100 countries engaging in collaborative learning, advocacy, and action. The 2026 Asia edition marks a significant milestone as the first regional camp hosted in Asia, offering a unique opportunity for regional voices to shape climate justice solutions. What is the Climate Justice Camp? The Climate Justice Camp is an annual global gathering designed to create a space where community organizers can: Build meaningful relationships and networks Share experiences, knowledge, and best practices Develop innovative strategies for climate action Strengthen grassroots movements and advocacy efforts It is a collaborative, participant-driven platform that emphasizes local knowledge, collective care, and transformative change. Event Details Location: Cebu, Philippines Dates: August 12–16, 2026 Arrival Date: August 11, 2026 Departure Date: August 17, 2026 The camp runs for a full week, and participants are expected to attend all sessions. What the Camp Offers Participants will gain access to a rich and immersive experience designed to foster learning, collaboration, and action. Key Features Workshops and Trainings Skill-building sessions led by experts and participants Topics include climate justice, advocacy, organizing, and policy Collaborative Learning Peer-to-peer knowledge sharing Exchange of ideas across diverse communities Networking Opportunities Connect with activists, leaders, and organizations Build long-term partnerships and alliances Strategy Development Co-create solutions to pressing climate challenges Develop actionable plans for local implementation Healing and Reflection Spaces Focus on resilience, well-being, and collective care Safe space for sharing experiences and challenges Financial Support and Costs The Climate Justice Camp ensures accessibility for participants by covering several key costs: Covered Expenses Accommodation Meals Local transportation within Cebu Participant Responsibilities Travel costs to Cebu Visa-related expenses (if required) Limited travel sponsorship is available and will be assessed on a case-by-case basis. Approved travel support will be reimbursed upon arrival. Eligibility Criteria The program is open to individuals who meet the following conditions: Must reside in the Asia region or be part of the Asian diaspora Residents of Pacific Island countries are also eligible Actively engaged in community organizing or advocacy work Working on issues such as: Climate justice Environmental protection Human rights Women’s rights Indigenous rights Water rights and sustainability Applicants are encouraged to apply as individuals or with members of their organization or community. Eligible Countries Applicants can apply from a wide range of countries across Asia and the Pacific, including but not limited to: South Asia: India, Pakistan, Nepal, Bangladesh, Sri Lanka Southeast Asia: Indonesia, Philippines, Vietnam, Thailand, Malaysia East Asia: China, Japan, South Korea Central Asia: Kazakhstan, Uzbekistan, Kyrgyzstan Pacific Islands: Fiji, Samoa, Tonga, Vanuatu, and others A comprehensive list of eligible countries is provided in the official application details. Application Deadline Deadline: May 31, 2026 (23:59 UTC+5) Applicants are encouraged to apply early due to the high volume of submissions. APPLY HERE Selection Timeline Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis Selected participants will be notified directly If no response is received by July 10, 2026, applicants should consider their application unsuccessful Why You Should Apply Participating in the Climate Justice Camp offers unparalleled opportunities to: Engage with leading climate activists and organizers Strengthen your skills in advocacy and organizing Contribute to global and regional climate solutions Build networks that extend beyond the camp Amplify your impact within your community Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs) 1. What is the Climate Justice Camp? It is a global platform for grassroots organizers to collaborate, learn, and develop strategies for climate and social justice. 2. Who can apply? Community organizers, activists, and leaders working on justice-related issues in Asia, the Pacific, or the Asian diaspora. 3. Can I apply with my team? Yes. Group applications are encouraged, but each member must submit an individual application. 4. Is the program fully funded? The camp covers accommodation, meals, and local transport. Travel and visa costs are the responsibility of participants, though limited funding is available. 5. Do I need to attend all days? Yes. Full participation is mandatory for all selected participants. 6. When will I know the results? Applicants will be contacted directly if selected or waitlisted. No response by July 10 indicates non-selection. 7. What should I expect during the camp? Workshops, networking, collaborative sessions, and opportunities to co-create solutions for climate justice. 8. Who organizes the camp? The initiative is hosted by Roots, in collaboration with over 200 global and regional partner organizations. Click HERE to learn more about the Climate Justice Camp 2026 Asia. Conclusion The Climate Justice Camp 2026 Asia is a powerful platform for emerging and experienced leaders to come together, share knowledge, and drive meaningful change. It represents a unique opportunity to strengthen grassroots movements and contribute to a more just and sustainable future. Discover more global Climate Action and Conference opportunities on OFY. title: International Paralympic Committee Classification Research Grant (2026–2028)
summary: The International Paralympic Committee has launched the second call for its Classification Research Grant , supporting multidisciplinary research that strengthens evidence-based classification systems across Para sports. About the Grant This funding opportunity supports research designed to improve fairness, transparency, and scientific rigor in Para sport classification. Projects should contribute to the development of robust classification systems that enhance competitive equity across Paralympic sports. Funding Details Grant Amount: €30,000 – €150,000 Project Duration: 12–24 months Programme Period: 2026–2028 Who Can Apply Eligible applicants include: International Federations National Paralympic Committees Research Institutions Collaborative partnerships between academic institutions and sport bodies Application Process The grant follows a two-stage application process , beginning with submission of a short project summary. Key Deadline Phase 1 Deadline: 31 May 2026 Why This Opportunity Matters This is a valuable opportunity for researchers and institutions working at the intersection of: Sport science Disability inclusion Performance research Evidence-based policy in sport It supports efforts to build fairer and more scientifically grounded Para sport systems worldwide. How to Apply Learn more and apply here For more Grant opportunities, Visit OFY here. title: Recourse Is Hiring an Advisor in Africa to Support Just Energy Transition Advocacy
summary: Global advocacy organization Recourse is hiring an Advisor based in Africa to support its research, advocacy, and campaigns focused on international financial institutions and just energy transitions. This is an exciting opportunity for professionals passionate about climate justice, development finance, and sustainable energy systems to contribute to global advocacy efforts while working remotely within an international team. Although the preferred location is Kenya, the role is open to candidates based across Africa and has a global scope. About the Role The Advisor will support Recourse’s strategic work influencing international financial institutions and advocating for equitable energy transition policies in the Global South. The successful candidate will collaborate with allies and partners across multiple regions, contributing to research, campaigns, and policy engagement initiatives. Key Responsibilities Responsibilities are expected to include: Supporting research on development finance and international financial institutions Contributing to advocacy and campaign strategies Engaging with partners and stakeholders across regions Advancing work on just energy transitions in Global South contexts Collaborating with remote global teams Undertaking occasional international travel (up to four weeks per year) Candidate Profile Recourse is seeking applicants with: Strong understanding of development finance Expertise in energy transitions , especially in Global South contexts Experience in advocacy, research, or campaigning Ability to work effectively in distributed international teams Strong communication and stakeholder engagement skills Location and Work Arrangement Primary Base: Africa Preferred Location: Kenya Work Style: Remote Travel Requirement: Up to four weeks annually Application Deadline No deadline has been specified. Early application is recommended. How to Apply To view the full job description and application process, visit here. For more Job opportunities, Visit OFY here.
